audience: professional banking/finance analysts (completeness, rigor, comparability, documented methodology)

axes.tier: T0 headline (executive answer) | T1 core CAMELS spine | T2 applied/comparative | T3 context/structure | T4 reference/ops
axes.story_sequence: S0..S15 — reading order in the sector-story arc (macro first, ops last); ORTHOGONAL to tier
axes.camels: C Capital | A Asset quality | M Management | E Earnings | L Liquidity | S Sensitivity to market risk | - n/a
axes.class: headline (answers the tab's question at a glance; <=2/tab) | supporting (a needed dimension) | depth (expert cut, below fold) | cut_candidate (low fit + redundant) | orphan (no spine theme / no registry metric / no source)
axes.audit_verdict: keep | reorder | merge | split | relocate | clarify_purpose | promote | demote | add_missing | add_to_nav
axes.rubric: question_fit(0-2,dominant) standard_view comparable_reproducible decision_relevance non_redundant (0-2) + scroll_earned sourced (bool)

spine.note: Exogenous to the banks but sets every other theme. Reproduced, not heavily interpreted.
The single most editorially load-bearing tab — the executive answer.
HOMED 2026-06-27 (was the audit's P0). New /market-risk tab off bank_audit_fx_position + bank_audit_repricing (deterministic §4 extractors) + securities MtM from existing OCI/BS.

fsi: the core set as one surface
spine.note: Risks being a recap of S5-S7 unless it earns a distinct question (the regulator's Table-15 scorecard, by bank-type).
Each must carry a banking-anchored question or it reads as orphan adjacency.

tabs.title: Overview
tabs.guiding_question: How is the Turkish banking sector doing right now — size, growth, and the headline health vitals?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: One screen that says whether the sector is growing, profitable, well-provisioned and well-capitalised — and where to look next.
tabs.reading_prereqs: None — this is the entry point.
known_gaps: No cross-domain synthesis / 'what changed this month' framing (the future narrative seam lands here first)
Sensitivity (S) vital absent from the headline set
tabs.audit_notes: The T0 tab and the natural home for the future narrative layer. Today it is a bare KPI+chart grid with a one-line data-source caption; no editorial lead.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: This month: where the sector accelerated, where stress is building, and which CAMELS vital moved most.

charts.title: Total Assets — Level (sector)
charts.audit_notes: Duplicates the entire /sector (root) page, which is itself a one-chart orphan not in Nav. Fold them.

charts.title: Loan Growth YoY by bank group

tabs.title: Credit
tabs.guiding_question: How fast is credit growing, in what currency, to whom — and how does that split between public and private banks?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: Credit momentum, its TL/FX mix, the consumer-vs-commercial composition, and the public/private divergence that drives the sector narrative.
known_gaps: fx_adjusted_loan_growth — BBVA's headline credit metric (13-week FX-adjusted annualized); we show nominal m/m and 4w-annualized only
'non-capped vs capped' loan-category lens (BBVA's April framing)
tabs.audit_notes: 18 charts — the densest sector tab and analytically rich. But the public-vs-private and TL/FX divergence (BBVA's lead) is buried in section 5; it should lead. Add FX-adjusted growth.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Credit growth held its trend-rate; the public/private split widened, led by TL lending into non-capped items.

charts.title: Credit Cards — Retail vs Corporate (Level, monthly)
Total Credit YoY — Public vs Private
charts.audit_notes: Its own description calls this 'the clearest sector signal' yet it sits 14th. BBVA's lead divergence. Promote to the top of the tab.

charts.title: TL Loans YoY — Public vs Private

tabs.title: Deposits
tabs.guiding_question: Where is the sector's funding coming from — is it growing, sticky (demand vs term), and is it dollarizing?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: Deposit growth by group, the demand/term and maturity mix, and the FX-share (dollarization) signal that drives funding cost and policy.
known_gaps: Time-deposit rate / cost-of-deposit context lives on Rates, not here
tabs.audit_notes: Dollarization (FX share) is the BBVA headline for deposits but sits at chart 12 (section 3). The sector-only 'Deposit YoY' (chart 9) duplicates the by-group YoY (chart 2).
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Deposits stayed in lira despite high rates; FX-share eased — dollarization under control.

charts.title: Deposit YoY — sector
charts.audit_notes: Redundant with chart 2 (by-group YoY already includes the sector line).

charts.title: FX Share of Total Deposits (%)
charts.audit_notes: Dollarization — the BBVA deposit headline. Promote to lead the tab.

charts.title: LDR (%) — by group
charts.audit_notes: LDR also on Overview and (TL/FX) on Liquidity — cross-tab repetition; assign one canonical home.

tabs.title: Asset Quality
tabs.guiding_question: Is the credit the sector extended actually good — and where is deterioration concentrated?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: NPL is low but creeping up; coverage stable; the leading edge is consumer cards/GPL. Stress is gradual, not a cliff.
known_gaps: stage2_ratio — sector SICR/Stage-2 series (BBVA's 'financial stress' lens); held per-bank in bank_audit_stages but not surfaced at sector level
restructured_loans_ratio
net_npl_formation / cost_of_risk at sector level
tabs.audit_notes: Solid on NPL+coverage. The biggest gap vs BBVA/FSR credit-risk chapter is Stage-2 / restructured-loan migration — the early-warning layer.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: NPL ticked up but coverage held; watch Stage-2 migration in retail.

tabs.title: Capital
tabs.guiding_question: Can the sector absorb losses — how much capital does it hold over the regulatory minimum, and what is driving the ratio?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: CAR comfortably above the 12% minimum but compressing as RWA (credit) grows and dividends are paid; CET1 detail only available per-bank.
known_gaps: CET1 / Tier-1 ratio sector series — the Basel headline; monthly bulletin carries only total CAR (financial_ratios item 30). Per-bank CET1 exists in bank_audit_capital
Basel leverage ratio (FSI encouraged) at sector level
tabs.audit_notes: CAR by group is right. Missing the CET1 decomposition the BBVA note leads with on capital; the audit lane already holds per-bank CET1.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Capital ratios slipped in March on dividends + RWA growth, but stayed well above minimums.

charts.title: Off-Balance-Sheet Derivatives / Total Assets (%)
charts.audit_notes: This is really a market-risk (S8/Sensitivity) signal sitting under Capital — symptom of the unhomed 'S'.

tabs.title: Profitability
tabs.guiding_question: Is the sector earning its cost of capital — and what is driving the margin and the return?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: ROE/ROA and the NIM bridge; fee contribution and cost efficiency; whether returns beat inflation (real ROE).
known_gaps: cost_income (the standard efficiency ratio) at sector level — shown as OPEX/assets + fee ratios instead; cross-bank has cost/income
tabs.audit_notes: Returns + margins + real-ROE are strong (real ROE is a BBVA-grade view). The three fee/cost-coverage ratios (Fees/Revenue, Non-int income/expense, Fees/OPEX) overlap — keep one headline, fold the rest to depth.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: ROE ran flat-to-lower vs 2025; margins thin as funding cost bites; fees cushion.

charts.title: ROE (annualized) vs CPI 12m avg (%)
charts.audit_notes: Real-ROE view — a BBVA-grade lens; keep and consider promoting.

tabs.title: Ratios
tabs.guiding_question: What does the BDDK's official ratio scorecard look like for a chosen bank-type aggregate?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: The regulator's Table-15 headline ratios, switchable across bank-type (Sector/State/Domestic/Foreign/Participation/Dev&Inv) — a like-for-like comparability surface.
fsi: the core set as one surface
known_gaps: No CAR or coverage in the scorecard (it stops at NPL/NIM/LDR/ROA/ROE)
tabs.audit_notes: Six KPI cards that overlap Overview + Profitability. Its ONLY distinct value is the bank-TYPE filter. Reframe explicitly as the by-bank-type Table-15 scorecard, or fold into Overview/Profitability with a type switch.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Compare the vitals across state vs private vs participation banks at a glance.

tabs.title: Liquidity
tabs.guiding_question: Can the sector fund itself — TL and FC loan/deposit pressure, dollarization, and the CBRT liquidity backdrop?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: TL/FC loan-to-deposit pressure, the dollarization of savings, and the CBRT funding/reserve stance that sets system liquidity.
known_gaps: LCR and NSFR — the two standard Basel liquidity ratios are ABSENT at sector level (held per-bank in bank_audit_liquidity). The 'Liquidity' tab has no liquidity-coverage ratio.
HQLA / liquid-assets-to-assets (FSI core)
tabs.audit_notes: Half the tab is liquidity (TL/FC LDR), half is macro/market-risk (REER, CBRT reserves, net funding, dollarization). It is silently absorbing the unhomed Sensitivity (S8) and macro overlap. Add LCR/NSFR; relocate REER to Economy; dedupe dollarization with Deposits.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: TL funding ample; FC LDR contained; CBRT in net-funding mode.

charts.title: TL Loan / Deposit Ratio (%) — public vs private
TL Deposit Growth YoY (%) — public vs private
FC Loan / Deposit Ratio (%) — public vs private
Deposit Dollarization — FC share of deposits (%)
charts.audit_notes: Duplicates Deposits 'FX Share of Total Deposits'. Keep one canonical dollarization chart.

charts.title: Net CBRT Funding (TL bn)
charts.audit_notes: Really S8/macro plumbing (BBVA lead). Verified by catalog.

charts.title: Real Effective Exchange Rate (2003=100)
charts.audit_notes: REER also on Economy. Relocate to the macro backdrop; not a liquidity metric.

tabs.title: Digital
tabs.guiding_question: How fast is banking migrating to digital channels — adoption, the customer funnel, and transaction volumes?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: Channel migration (mobile has replaced internet), the registered-base funnel, remote vs branch acquisition, and digital transaction dominance.
known_gaps: Franchise metrics are non-standard across banks (TBB sums per-bank registrations; multi-bank customers double-count) — already caveated in section descriptions
Nav-grouped under Sector but it is structural context, not a CAMELS vital
tabs.audit_notes: Strong, well-caveated TBB lane. But it sits in the 'Sector' nav group between the CAMELS vitals; it belongs in a structure/context group (with Funds/Non-Bank), not between Profitability and Liquidity.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Mobile-only banking is the norm; remote onboarding keeps gaining share.

charts.title: Active individuals by channel usage (% of total)
Net new registered customers per quarter (millions)
Product applications via mobile per quarter (millions)
Share of new customers by channel (%)
New individual customers by acquisition method (thousands)
Active individuals by gender (% of total)

tabs.title: Banks
tabs.guiding_question: Which banks are there, how big are they, and where do I drill into one?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: The bank universe grouped by BDDK type and sized by assets — the index into per-bank analysis.
tabs.audit_notes: A directory, not an analytic tab — correctly so. No charts.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Entry point to per-bank drill-downs.

tabs.title: Bank detail
tabs.guiding_question: For this one bank: how does it perform, how is it built, and who owns it?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: A full per-bank CAMELS drill-down: profile, valuation, margin/return performance, the three statements, ownership and disclosures.
known_gaps: No per-bank capital (CET1/CAR) or liquidity (LCR/NSFR) section, though bank_audit_capital / _liquidity hold them — the per-bank 'C' and 'L' are thin vs the rich 'E' (performance) section
tabs.audit_notes: The most complete surface in the app (profile, market&valuation, performance/margins, BS/IS/CF + Sankey, ownership, disclosures). Gap: per-bank Capital & Liquidity sections despite the data existing.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: This bank's edge (or weakness) vs peers, in one page.

charts.title: Margin bridge — loan yield vs deposit cost (TTM)

tabs.title: Compare
tabs.guiding_question: How does each bank rank against its peers across the full performance set?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: The comparability surface: every bank scored vs peers on the FSI-core set (NPL, coverage, ROE/ROA/NIM, cost/income, P/B-P/E) plus market share & concentration.
tabs.reading_prereqs: The sector tabs (defines the metrics being compared).
known_gaps: No capital (CAR/CET1) or liquidity (LCR) column in the heatmap — same C/L thinness as the per-bank page
tabs.audit_notes: The strongest comparability surface in the app and the de-facto per-bank FSI core set. High value; keep and consider adding CAR/CET1 columns once the per-bank capital lane is surfaced.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Who leads and who lags this quarter, and is the sector concentrating?

tabs.title: Valuation
tabs.guiding_question: Are the listed banks cheap or expensive — what is each worth on fundamentals vs the market price?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: Forward intrinsic value (residual income / DDM / justified P/B) vs market, with editable scenarios and a peer P/B-vs-ROE map.
known_gaps: Cost of equity inputs (ERP/beta) are assumptions, not sourced — appropriately caveated
tabs.audit_notes: A genuine analyst tool (RI/DDM/justified-P/B + scenarios + peer regression). Only listed banks (11). Keep.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Where the market is mispricing ROE durability.

charts.title: ROE fade vs cost of equity

tabs.title: Ownership
tabs.guiding_question: Who owns the banks, what do they own, and where do ownership webs connect banks?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: The sector ownership network — shareholders, subsidiaries and cross-bank stakes — as a governance/structure lens (CAMELS 'M' proxy).
known_gaps: Stakes are point-in-time KAP filings; no time dimension
tabs.audit_notes: Distinctive interactive network; the closest the app gets to CAMELS 'M' (Management/governance) for an external analyst. Keep.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: State vs private vs foreign control, and the cross-holding web.

tabs.title: Rates
tabs.guiding_question: What is the monetary-policy and rate backdrop the banks operate against?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: Policy/funding rates and the corridor, FX, the lending/deposit-rate survey, and CBRT sterilization — the price of money.
known_gaps: Catalog spec 'rates.cbrt_foreign_asset_share' (CBRT balance-sheet section) is defined but NOT rendered on the page — a catalog/UI reconciliation gap
tabs.audit_notes: Macro plumbing; overlaps Economy (USD also there). Dual-homed S0 backdrop + an input to the (missing) S8 sensitivity story.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Policy held at 40%; corridor and FX set the funding-cost floor.

charts.title: Rate Corridor — Policy + ON + Effective Funding (%)

tabs.title: Market Risk
tabs.guiding_question: How exposed is the sector to FX and interest-rate shocks — net open FX position, repricing gap, and securities mark-to-market?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: The CAMELS 'S' vital: FX net open position vs capital (well-hedged or not), the interest-rate repricing gap, and the AFS/FVOCI securities reserve — the market-risk lens a dollarized banking system lives or dies by.
tabs.reading_prereqs: Rates (the shock source) + Capital (the buffer the position is sized against).
known_gaps: Repricing gap ~81% coverage — participation banks word it as profit-share rate and often omit it (validated N/A)
Sector view aggregates 'of reporting banks' (per-bank audit, no monthly sector FX-NOP series)
tabs.audit_notes: NEW tab (2026-06-27) resolving the audit's P0 'CAMELS S unhomed'. Off the new bank_audit_fx_position + bank_audit_repricing extractors; securities MtM reuses bank_audit_oci/BS. Placed in Markets & Macro next to Rates.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: FX position well inside the NOP limit; the repricing gap is where a rate move bites NIM.

tabs.title: Funds
tabs.guiding_question: How much savings has migrated into investment funds, and what do those funds hold?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: Fund-market AUM by type/category, portfolio allocation, investor counts — the disintermediated-savings flow that competes with deposits.
known_gaps: Banking anchor (fund AUM vs deposits) is implicit, not charted
tabs.audit_notes: Strong TEFAS lane. Adjacency to banking via deposit substitution; make that link explicit to earn its place.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Money-market funds keep absorbing deposit migration.

charts.title: Assets under management by fund type (TL trn)
Mutual-fund AUM by category (% of total)
Mutual-fund portfolio allocation (% of covered AUM)

tabs.title: Non-Bank
tabs.guiding_question: How big are the non-bank lenders (leasing/factoring/financing) that compete with banks for credit?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: The non-bank lending sector's size and lending book, by segment — the shadow of bank credit.
known_gaps: VYŞ + savings-finance segments deferred (sparse feed)
tabs.audit_notes: Same-source (BDDK) NBFI aggregate. Adjacency context; keep.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Non-bank credit stays a small but growing slice.

charts.title: Non-bank sector assets / Share / Lending book (KPIs)

tabs.title: Share of Banking
tabs.guiding_question: How much of total system credit is done outside the banks (disintermediation)?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: Non-bank share of system assets and credit over time, and per segment vs bank loans.
tabs.audit_notes: The disintermediation measure; apples-to-apples (both BDDK). Keep.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Disintermediation creeps up but stays ~3% of bank assets.

tabs.title: Economy
tabs.guiding_question: What macro backdrop — growth, labor, inflation/policy, external balance, fiscal — are the banks operating in?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: The one-screen macro context that drives credit demand, asset quality and rates, reproduced from the BBVA outlook structure.
known_gaps: Heaviest-invested lane in the app (hub + 5 deep sub-pages) — arguably deeper than the banking lanes it contextualizes (balance note, not a defect)
tabs.audit_notes: The narrative-rich precedent: multi-sentence section descriptions with report-page citations. The future narrative layer should match this voice. Keep; it is the model.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Disinflation is slow, the lira is appreciating in real terms, fiscal has room.

charts.title: CPI Inflation vs CBRT Effective Funding Cost (%)
Current Account Balance (12m rolling, USD bn)
Net Errors & Omissions (12m rolling, USD bn)
General Budget Balances (12m rolling, % of GDP)
BBVA Baseline Scenario
charts.audit_notes: Curated forecast table — a non-reproducible external snapshot; the curated-narrative precedent alongside the deterministic charts.

tabs.title: Economic Growth
tabs.guiding_question: How fast is the real economy growing and what is driving it (expenditure & production sides)?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: GDP growth, demand-side contributions and sectoral value added — the credit-demand backdrop.
known_gaps: q/q SA series and some expenditure detail come from a TÜİK Excel ingest, not EVDS
tabs.audit_notes: Deep Albaraka/BBVA-report reproduction; catalog-backed.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Domestic demand carries growth; net exports drag.

tabs.title: Balance of Payments
tabs.guiding_question: Is Türkiye's external position financeable — the current account and how the deficit is funded?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: Current account (incl. core ex gold/energy), capital inflows, weekly foreign portfolio flows, and the financing identity.
tabs.audit_notes: Very deep (10 Şekil + weekly portfolio flows + summary); among the richest macro lanes.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: External financing leans on portfolio + reserves; core balance is the tell.

charts.title: Şekil 10 · Financing of the Current Account

tabs.title: Budget
tabs.guiding_question: What is the fiscal stance — the central-government balance and the revenue/expenditure mix?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: Annualized budget & primary balance, revenue and expenditure composition — fiscal room against shocks.
tabs.audit_notes: Albaraka «Bütçe» reproduction; catalog-backed.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Primary surplus gives fiscal room.

tabs.title: Inflation
tabs.guiding_question: What is the inflation picture — headline, core, and the group/sector breakdown?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: CPI/PPI headline and core-C, COICOP group contributions and PPI sub-sectors — the disinflation read.
known_gaps: Group weights not in EVDS → contributions shown as m/m; MIG table from TÜİK bulletin ingest
tabs.audit_notes: Albaraka «Enflasyon» reproduction; catalog-backed.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Core sticky above target; expectations turning up.

tabs.title: Foreign Trade
tabs.guiding_question: What is happening to the trade balance and its energy/terms-of-trade drivers?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: Trade balance (incl. ex-energy), exports/imports, BEC product mix, terms of trade and the energy deficit.
known_gaps: HS-chapter detail not reproducible (proprietary/TÜİK dynamic DB)
tabs.audit_notes: Albaraka «Dış Ticaret» reproduction; catalog-backed.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Energy bill drives the trade gap; terms of trade swing with Brent.

tabs.title: Regulation
tabs.guiding_question: What macroprudential rules are in force and what changed recently?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: A standing snapshot of the rule-set (loan caps, reserve ratios, deposit rules, CARs) plus the raw TCMB/BDDK feed.
tabs.audit_notes: The ONE existing narrative precedent: a weekly Kimi-generated briefing (regulation_briefings) rendered as category cards + sourced bullets, with no LLM on page load. The template a future narrative layer can reuse.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: What macroprudential setting changed and how it bites the banks.

charts.title: Current Regulatory Snapshot (briefing)
charts.audit_notes: LLM-generated weekly; the reusable narrative pattern.

tabs.title: Sector Press
tabs.guiding_question: What is the banking-sector press saying this week?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: Keyword-filtered banking coverage from Turkish financial media.
tabs.audit_notes: Press feed; reference.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: The week's catalysts and headlines.

tabs.title: Google News
tabs.guiding_question: What is the long tail of banking news (beyond curated press)?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: Google-News-sourced coverage, deduped against Sector Press.
tabs.audit_notes: Long-tail news; reference.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Regional/trade-outlet long tail.

tabs.title: Pipeline
tabs.guiding_question: Where does each number come from and how fresh is it?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: The end-to-end data lineage with live freshness + run status — the methodology-transparency surface analysts value.
tabs.audit_notes: React Flow lineage graph; provenance. Keep.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Provenance and freshness at a glance.

tabs.title: Admin
tabs.guiding_question: (Operational) Is the pipeline healthy and how do I trigger a refresh?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: Internal control center — not part of the analytic narrative.
tabs.audit_notes: Ops only; password-gated. Out of the analytic spine.

tabs.title: Disclosures
tabs.guiding_question: What have the listed banks filed on KAP recently?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: All KAP bank filings, newest first (also reachable per-bank).
known_gaps: NOT linked in Nav — reachable only via /banks links or direct URL
tabs.audit_notes: Orphaned from navigation. Either add under 'News' (or 'By Bank'), or fold into News. A real page with no nav entry is undiscoverable.
tabs.future_narrative_hook: Material per-bank events as they file.

tabs.title: Total Assets (sector root)
tabs.guiding_question: (Unclear) What does sector total-assets look like over time?
tabs.analyst_takeaway: A single Total-Assets area chart — duplicated by the Overview tab.
known_gaps: NOT in Nav; single chart duplicates Overview's 'Total Assets — Level'
tabs.audit_notes: Orphan one-chart page, not in Nav, redundant with Overview. The /sector/ratios child IS linked but the /sector root is a dangling page. Fold into Overview or remove.
